refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR uses 'support achieving' which can be ambiguous and potentially implies implementation. Replacing it with 'enable achieving' keeps the focus on the system's functional capability without specifying how. The FR is atomic as it addresses a single obligation: achieving the energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the energy_per_ton metric (energy_used divided by ton_moved) over the agreed evaluation period and verify it meets or is below the customer-defined target E_target.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined energy efficiency target.

candidate_dps: Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control
Optimize operational parameters to minimize energy per ton moved
Incorporate adaptive load management to balance energy use
Design energy recovery mechanisms to reuse energy within the system
Establish a configurable energy efficiency target parameter for system tuning
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control

potential_sources: Energy monitoring tightly coupled with control logic
Feedback loop dependencies on accurate load measurement
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on monitoring and control as a logical unit, which can be modularized to maintain independence from other system functions. Potential coupling arises if energy measurement and control are not well encapsulated, but this can be managed by clear interface definitions.

assumptions_risks: Accurate and timely energy measurement is feasible within system constraints
Operational policies allow for dynamic control adjustments
Energy efficiency target E_target is well-defined and stable during evaluation period
Risk that measurement errors or delays could degrade control effectiveness
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining the interface and data flow between energy monitoring and control modules to ensure modularity and reduce coupling risk.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for energy management and monitoring within the system

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides real-time data on energy consumption and system status for monitoring and feedback control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Enables communication with other system modules and external services for control commands and data exchange.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as energy monitoring and feedback logically belong here; minimal diffusion as most related functions are internal.
Partial containment since energy consumption relates to vehicle operation, but feedback control may require external data, causing moderate diffusion.
Low containment; teleoperation focuses on remote control, not energy monitoring, leading to high diffusion if included here.
Safety services may use energy data for alerts but do not contain the core monitoring and control logic, causing diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the DP as it specializes in energy management functions, ensuring high cohesion and low diffusion of the real-time energy monitoring and feedback control functionality.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for raw energy data may cause minor coupling.
Integration with Telemetry and Network Link interfaces requires careful interface management to avoid unintended diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control' directly addresses the FR of enabling achievement of the customer-defined energy efficiency target by providing the necessary monitoring and control mechanisms to meet the acceptance criteria of energy_per_ton ≤ E_target.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for raw energy data may cause minor coupling.
Integration with Telemetry and Network Link interfaces requires careful interface management to avoid unintended diffusion.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and low diffusion scores, indicating it is an appropriate IM boundary for the DP. Most related functions are internal, minimizing diffusion risk. However, minor coupling with the Vehicle module and interface management with Telemetry and Network Link must be carefully handled to maintain containment.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the acceptance criteria specify measurable energy efficiency targets (energy_per_ton ≤ E_target) that can be monitored via the implemented real-time monitoring and feedback control within the NGHE Solution module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and justified by the module's specialization in energy management and monitoring, supported by high containment and low diffusion scores.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly define interface contracts with the Vehicle module to minimize coupling and clarify data ownership for raw energy data.
Establish rigorous interface management protocols for Telemetry and Network Link to prevent unintended diffusion of DP functionality across modules.
Consider documenting fallback or error handling strategies within the NGHE Solution module to ensure robustness in case of data unavailability from external interfaces.

decision.justification: Although all gates have passed indicating that the current DP and IM adequately address the FR with feasible verification and clear allocation, the high safety criticality and identified minor coupling and diffusion risks necessitate revisions. Specifically, explicit interface contracts and rigorous interface management protocols must be defined to minimize coupling and prevent unintended diffusion. Additionally, documenting fallback or error handling strategies is essential to ensure robustness and safety compliance.

revise.why: To explicitly define interface contracts with the Vehicle module and establish rigorous interface management protocols for Telemetry and Network Link interfaces, as well as to document fallback or error handling strategies within the NGHE Solution module to mitigate diffusion risks and enhance robustness.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module is specialized and appropriate for implementing the DP.
Energy efficiency targets are measurable and verifiable via the implemented monitoring and control.
Minor coupling and diffusion risks can be mitigated through interface management.
open_risks: Potential coupling with Vehicle module for raw energy data.
Risk of unintended diffusion through Telemetry and Network Link interfaces.
Robustness in case of data unavailability from external interfaces.
evidence_needed: Verification test results confirming energy efficiency targets are met under normal and fallback conditions.
